 {"id":"bd-1mh","title":"Update session picker to use SQLite index","description":"# Update session picker to use SQLite index

## Goal
Replace filesystem walking in session picker with fast SQLite index queries.

## Current Implementation (src/session_picker.rs)
Currently scans filesystem:
```rust
// Slow: walks all session directories
let sessions = walkdir::WalkDir::new(&sessions_dir)
    .into_iter()
    .filter_map(|e| e.ok())
    .filter(|e| e.path().extension() == Some("jsonl"))
    .collect();
```

## New Implementation
```rust
use crate::session_index::SessionIndex;

impl SessionPicker {
    pub fn load_sessions(cwd: Option<&str>) -> Result<Vec<SessionMeta>> {
        let index = SessionIndex::open_default()?;
        index.list_sessions(cwd)
    }
}
```

## UI Changes
- Display indexed metadata directly (no file reads needed)
- Show message count from index
- Show session name from index
- Faster render (no filesystem I/O)

## Fallback Behavior
If index unavailable or empty:
```rust
fn load_sessions_with_fallback(cwd: Option<&str>) -> Result<Vec<SessionMeta>> {
    // Try index first
    if let Ok(index) = SessionIndex::open_default() {
        if let Ok(sessions) = index.list_sessions(cwd) {
            if !sessions.is_empty() {
                return Ok(sessions);
            }
        }
    }
    
    // Fallback to filesystem (and populate index)
    let sessions = load_sessions_from_filesystem(cwd)?;
    // Optionally trigger reindex in background
    Ok(sessions)
}
```

## Dependencies
- bd-3nz (indexing must work first)

## Testing
- Test: Session picker shows indexed sessions
- Test: Picker falls back to filesystem if needed
- Test: Performance improved (measure time)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Picker queries index by default
- [ ] Fallback to filesystem works
- [ ] UI displays index metadata
- [ ] Noticeably faster for 50+ sessions","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:38:46.861448590Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T03:38:59.101819926Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1mh","depends_on_id":"bd-346","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:38:46.870643664Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1mh","depends_on_id":"bd-3nz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:38:59.101795250Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

-{"id":"bd-1wc","title":"Implement CLI E2E integration tests with verbose logging","description":"# Implement CLI E2E integration tests with verbose logging

## Goal
Create end-to-end tests that invoke the pi binary directly and verify behavior,
with detailed logging for debugging failures.

## Background
src/main.rs (1581 lines) and src/cli.rs (332 lines) have minimal testing.
The CLI is the primary user interface and needs comprehensive E2E tests.

## Scope Boundary (Important)
- This bead builds the CLI E2E harness + offline CLI flag coverage.
- Provider-dependent scenarios live in:
  - bd-1ub (print mode + stdin)
  - bd-idw (session lifecycle)
  - bd-1o4 (tool enable/disable + error paths)

## Approach

### Test Harness
```rust
// tests/e2e_cli.rs

use std::process::{Command, Stdio};
use std::time::Instant;

struct CliTestHarness {
    binary_path: PathBuf,
    temp_dir: TempDir,
    env: HashMap<String, String>,
    logs: Vec<LogEntry>,
}

struct LogEntry {
    timestamp: Instant,
    level: LogLevel,
    message: String,
    context: HashMap<String, String>,
}

impl CliTestHarness {
    fn new() -> Self {
        let binary = cargo_bin("pi");
        let temp = TempDir::new().unwrap();
        Self {
            binary_path: binary,
            temp_dir: temp,
            env: HashMap::new(),
            logs: Vec::new(),
        }
    }

    fn log(&mut self, level: LogLevel, msg: &str, ctx: &[(&str, &str)]) {
        self.logs.push(LogEntry {
            timestamp: Instant::now(),
            level,
            message: msg.to_string(),
            context: ctx.iter().map(|(k,v)| (k.to_string(), v.to_string())).collect(),
        });
    }

    fn run(&mut self, args: &[&str]) -> CliResult {
        self.log(Info, "Starting CLI", &[("args", &args.join(" "))]);

        let start = Instant::now();
        let output = Command::new(&self.binary_path)
            .args(args)
            .envs(&self.env)
            .current_dir(self.temp_dir.path())
            .output()
            .expect("Failed to run pi");

        let duration = start.elapsed();
        self.log(Info, "CLI completed", &[
            ("exit_code", &output.status.code().unwrap_or(-1).to_string()),
            ("duration_ms", &duration.as_millis().to_string()),
            ("stdout_len", &output.stdout.len().to_string()),
            ("stderr_len", &output.stderr.len().to_string()),
        ]);

        CliResult {
            exit_code: output.status.code().unwrap_or(-1),
            stdout: String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout).to_string(),
            stderr: String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr).to_string(),
            duration,
        }
    }

    fn dump_logs(&self) -> String {
        // Format all logs for debugging
    }
}
```

## Logging Requirements
- Use TestLogger (bd-3ml) in the harness so failures auto-dump logs.
- Log: command, env (redacted), cwd, duration, stdout/stderr snapshot, and artifact paths.
- Attach logs + outputs as artifacts on failure (text snapshots).

## Determinism Requirements
- Offline-only tests here (help/version/list/config). Any provider-dependent test must live in sub-beads and use VCR playback.
- Ensure environment is fully controlled (PI_CONFIG_PATH, PI_SESSIONS_DIR).

## Test Categories (Offline)

### 1. Basic Invocation Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_version_flag() {
    let mut harness = CliTestHarness::new();
    let result = harness.run(&["--version"]);
    assert_eq!(result.exit_code, 0);
    assert!(result.stdout.contains("pi "));
    assert!(result.duration < Duration::from_millis(100));
}

#[test]
fn test_help_flag() {
    let mut harness = CliTestHarness::new();
    let result = harness.run(&["--help"]);
    assert_eq!(result.exit_code, 0);
    assert!(result.stdout.contains("Usage:"));
}

#[test]
fn test_invalid_flag() {
    let mut harness = CliTestHarness::new();
    let result = harness.run(&["--invalid-flag"]);
    assert_ne!(result.exit_code, 0);
    assert!(result.stderr.contains("error"));
}
```

### 2. Model Listing Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_list_models() {
    let mut harness = CliTestHarness::new();
    let result = harness.run(&["--list-models"]);
    assert_eq!(result.exit_code, 0);
    assert!(result.stdout.contains("claude"));
}

#[test]
fn test_list_models_filter() {
    let mut harness = CliTestHarness::new();
    let result = harness.run(&["--list-models", "opus"]);
    assert_eq!(result.exit_code, 0);
    assert!(result.stdout.contains("opus"));
    assert!(!result.stdout.contains("sonnet"));
}
```

src/main.rs (1581 lines) and src/cli.rs (332 lines) have minimal testing.
The CLI is the primary user interface and needs comprehensive E2E tests.

## Scope Boundary (Important)
- This bead builds the CLI E2E harness + offline CLI flag coverage.
- Provider-dependent scenarios live in:
  - bd-1ub (print mode + stdin)
  - bd-idw (session lifecycle)
  - bd-1o4 (tool enable/disable + error paths)

## Approach

### Test Harness
```rust
// tests/e2e_cli.rs

use std::process::{Command, Stdio};
use std::time::Instant;

struct CliTestHarness {
    binary_path: PathBuf,
    temp_dir: TempDir,
    env: HashMap<String, String>,
    logs: Vec<LogEntry>,
}

struct LogEntry {
    timestamp: Instant,
    level: LogLevel,
    message: String,
    context: HashMap<String, String>,
}

impl CliTestHarness {
    fn new() -> Self {
        let binary = cargo_bin("pi");
        let temp = TempDir::new().unwrap();
        Self {
            binary_path: binary,
            temp_dir: temp,
            env: HashMap::new(),
            logs: Vec::new(),
        }
    }

    fn log(&mut self, level: LogLevel, msg: &str, ctx: &[(&str, &str)]) {
        self.logs.push(LogEntry {
            timestamp: Instant::now(),
            level,
            message: msg.to_string(),
            context: ctx.iter().map(|(k,v)| (k.to_string(), v.to_string())).collect(),
        });
    }

    fn run(&mut self, args: &[&str]) -> CliResult {
        self.log(Info, "Starting CLI", &[("args", &args.join(" "))]);

        let start = Instant::now();
        let output = Command::new(&self.binary_path)
            .args(args)
            .envs(&self.env)
            .current_dir(self.temp_dir.path())
            .output()
            .expect("Failed to run pi");

        let duration = start.elapsed();
        self.log(Info, "CLI completed", &[
            ("exit_code", &output.status.code().unwrap_or(-1).to_string()),
            ("duration_ms", &duration.as_millis().to_string()),
            ("stdout_len", &output.stdout.len().to_string()),
            ("stderr_len", &output.stderr.len().to_string()),
        ]);

        CliResult {
            exit_code: output.status.code().unwrap_or(-1),
            stdout: String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout).to_string(),
            stderr: String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr).to_string(),
            duration,
        }
    }

    fn dump_logs(&self) -> String {
        // Format all logs for debugging
    }
}
```

## Logging Requirements
- Use TestLogger (bd-3ml) in the harness so failures auto-dump logs.
- Log: command, env (redacted), cwd, duration, stdout/stderr snapshot, and artifact paths.
- Attach logs + outputs as artifacts on failure (text snapshots).

## Determinism Requirements
- Offline-only tests here (help/version/list/config). Any provider-dependent test must live in sub-beads and use VCR playback.
- Ensure environment is fully controlled (PI_CONFIG_PATH, PI_SESSIONS_DIR).

## Test Categories (Offline)

### 1. Basic Invocation Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_version_flag() {
    let mut harness = CliTestHarness::new();
    let result = harness.run(&["--version"]);
    assert_eq!(result.exit_code, 0);
    assert!(result.stdout.contains("pi "));
    assert!(result.duration < Duration::from_millis(100));
}

#[test]
fn test_help_flag() {
    let mut harness = CliTestHarness::new();
    let result = harness.run(&["--help"]);
    assert_eq!(result.exit_code, 0);
    assert!(result.stdout.contains("Usage:"));
}

#[test]
fn test_invalid_flag() {
    let mut harness = CliTestHarness::new();
    let result = harness.run(&["--invalid-flag"]);
    assert_ne!(result.exit_code, 0);
    assert!(result.stderr.contains("error"));
}
```

### 2. Model Listing Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_list_models() {
    let mut harness = CliTestHarness::new();
    let result = harness.run(&["--list-models"]);
    assert_eq!(result.exit_code, 0);
    assert!(result.stdout.contains("claude"));
}

#[test]
fn test_list_models_filter() {
    let mut harness = CliTestHarness::new();
    let result = harness.run(&["--list-models", "opus"]);
    assert_eq!(result.exit_code, 0);
    assert!(result.stdout.contains("opus"));
    assert!(!result.stdout.contains("sonnet"));
}
```

### 3. Subcommand Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_list_packages() {
```

## Goal
Create recorded cassettes for all Anthropic provider test scenarios.

## Scenarios to Record

### Happy Path Scenarios
1. **Simple text response** - Single text block, stop reason "stop"
2. **Multi-paragraph response** - Multiple text deltas
3. **Extended thinking** - thinking_start/delta/end + text response
4. **Tool call single** - Single tool_call in response
5. **Tool call multiple** - Multiple tool_calls in single response
6. **Tool result processing** - tool_result message handling

### Error Scenarios
7. **Rate limit (429)** - With retry-after header
8. **Auth failure (401)** - Invalid API key
9. **Forbidden (403)** - Resource access denied
10. **Bad request (400)** - Malformed request
11. **Server error (500)** - Internal server error
12. **Overloaded (529)** - Anthropic overloaded

### Edge Cases
13. **Empty response** - No content blocks
14. **Very long response** - Hits max_tokens
15. **Unicode content** - Emoji, CJK, RTL text
16. **Large tool call args** - Big JSON in arguments
17. **Stream interruption** - Partial response simulation

### Extended Thinking Variants
18. **Thinking only** - No final text, just thinking
19. **Thinking with tool calls** - Thinking then tool use
20. **Thinking budget exceeded** - Hits thinking token limit

 {"id":"bd-346","title":"Workstream: Session Index Integration","description":"# Workstream: Session Index Integration

## Goal
Wire the existing SQLite session index (src/session_index.rs) into the CLI flow
for fast session discovery and resumption.

## Background
The session index module is fully implemented (360+ lines) but not used:
- SQLite-based index with WAL mode
- Fast lookups by path or CWD
- Session metadata extraction (message count, name, file size, mtime)
- Atomic operations with file locking

Currently, session operations scan the filesystem directly, which is slow
for users with many sessions.

## Current State

### What Exists (src/session_index.rs)
```rust
pub struct SessionIndex { db: SqliteConnection }

impl SessionIndex {
    pub fn open(path: &Path) -> Result<Self>
    pub fn index_session(&self, session_path: &Path) -> Result<()>
    pub fn list_sessions(&self, cwd: Option<&str>) -> Result<Vec<SessionMeta>>
    pub fn find_recent(&self, cwd: &str) -> Result<Option<SessionMeta>>
    pub fn reindex_all(&self) -> Result<()>
}

pub struct SessionMeta {
    pub path: PathBuf,
    pub cwd: String,
    pub name: Option<String>,
    pub message_count: u32,
    pub created_at: DateTime,
    pub modified_at: DateTime,
    pub file_size: u64,
}
```

### What's Missing
1. No calls to `index_session()` after session.persist()
2. No calls to `list_sessions()` in session picker
3. No calls to `find_recent()` for --continue flag
4. No index maintenance hooks

## Implementation Plan

### Phase 1: Wire Index on Session Save
- After session.persist(), call session_index.index_session()
- Index path stored at ~/.pi/agent/sessions/index.db

### Phase 2: Use Index for Session Listing
- --resume flag queries index instead of filesystem walk
- Session picker uses indexed metadata
- Much faster for users with 100+ sessions

### Phase 3: Use Index for --continue
- --continue queries index for most recent by CWD
- Falls back to filesystem if index empty/stale

### Phase 4: Index Maintenance
- Background reindex on startup (if stale)
- Handle deleted sessions (prune index)
- Handle moved sessions (update paths)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Sessions indexed on save
- [ ] Session picker uses index
- [ ] --continue uses index
- [ ] Startup remains fast (<100ms)
- [ ] Handles index corruption gracefully

## Files Affected
- src/session_index.rs (already implemented)
- src/session.rs (add indexing calls)
- src/session_picker.rs (use index queries)
- src/main.rs (wire up index)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:38:19.943589682Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T03:38:19.950957211Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-346","depends_on_id":"bd-2qk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:38:19.950924399Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3d8","title":"Implement /theme slash command","description":"# Implement /theme slash command

## Goal
Add /theme command to list available themes and switch between them.

## Command Syntax
```
/theme              - List available themes
/theme dark         - Switch to dark theme
/theme ocean-dark   - Switch to custom theme
```

## Implementation

### Command Handler
```rust
// src/interactive.rs

fn handle_theme_command(&mut self, args: &str) -> Cmd {
    if args.is_empty() {
        // List themes
        let themes = Theme::discover_themes();
        let current = &self.theme.name;
        
        let mut output = String::from("Available themes:\n");
        for path in themes {
            if let Ok(theme) = Theme::load(&path) {
                let marker = if theme.name == *current { "* " } else { "  " };
                output.push_str(&format!("{}{}\n", marker, theme.name));
            }
        }
        output.push_str("\nUse /theme <name> to switch");
        
        self.status_message = Some(output);
        Cmd::None
    } else {
        // Switch theme
        match Theme::load_by_name(args.trim()) {
            Ok(theme) => {
                self.theme = theme.clone();
                self.styles = create_styles(&theme);
                self.status_message = Some(format!("Switched to theme: {}", theme.name));
                
                // Persist preference
                if let Err(e) = self.save_theme_preference(&theme.name) {
                    tracing::warn!("Failed to save theme preference: {}", e);
                }
                
                Cmd::None
            }
            Err(_) => {
                self.status_message = Some(format!("Theme not found: {}", args.trim()));
                Cmd::None
            }
        }
    }
}
```

### Persist Theme Preference
```rust
fn save_theme_preference(&self, theme_name: &str) -> Result<()> {
    // Update settings.json with theme preference
    let settings_path = Config::settings_path();
    let mut settings = Config::load_from_file(&settings_path)?;
    settings.theme = Some(theme_name.to_string());
    settings.save_to_file(&settings_path)?;
    Ok(())
}
```

### Add to Slash Command Router
```rust
fn handle_slash_command(&mut self, input: &str) -> Cmd {
    let (cmd, args) = parse_slash_command(input);
    
    match cmd {
        "theme" => self.handle_theme_command(args),
        "help" => self.handle_help_command(),
        // ... other commands
    }
}
```

### Update /help Output
```
Commands:
  /help      - Show this help
  /model     - Switch model
  /thinking  - Set thinking level
  /theme     - List/switch themes  <-- NEW
  /clear     - Clear conversation
  /exit      - Exit Pi
```

 {"id":"bd-3ml","title":"Implement verbose test logging infrastructure","description":"# Implement verbose test logging infrastructure

## Goal
Create shared infrastructure for detailed logging in all integration tests,
enabling easy debugging when tests fail.

## Background
E2E and integration tests need detailed logging to debug failures:
- Every test action should be logged
- Timestamps for timing analysis
- Context (file paths, values) for reproduction
- Logs dumped automatically on test failure

## Implementation

### Log Infrastructure
```rust
// tests/common/logging.rs

use std::sync::Mutex;
use std::time::Instant;

#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct LogEntry {
    pub timestamp: Instant,
    pub level: LogLevel,
    pub category: &'static str,
    pub message: String,
    pub context: Vec<(String, String)>,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
pub enum LogLevel {
    Debug,
    Info,
    Warn,
    Error,
}

pub struct TestLogger {
    entries: Mutex<Vec<LogEntry>>,
    start: Instant,
}
```

### Redaction + Artifacts
- Add helpers to redact sensitive values in env/header/context keys (API keys, auth headers).
- Add a simple artifact registry: `record_artifact(name, path)` so tests can attach outputs.
- On panic, dump logs + artifact list (paths) to stderr.

### Test Harness Integration
```rust
// tests/common/harness.rs

pub struct TestHarness {
    pub logger: TestLogger,
    pub temp_dir: TempDir,
}

impl Drop for TestHarness {
    fn drop(&mut self) {
        if std::thread::panicking() {
            eprintln!("\n=== TEST LOGS ===\n{}", self.logger.dump());
            eprintln!("=== ARTIFACTS ===\n{}", self.logger.dump_artifacts());
        }
    }
}
```

### Optional File Output
- If `TEST_LOG_PATH` is set, write logs to that path in addition to stderr.

 {"id":"bd-3nz","title":"Wire session indexing into session.persist()","description":"# Wire session indexing into session.persist()

## Goal
Automatically update the SQLite index when sessions are saved.

## Implementation

### Add Index Update to persist()
```rust
// src/session.rs

impl Session {
    pub async fn persist(&self, path: &Path) -> Result<()> {
        // Existing JSONL write logic
        self.write_jsonl(path).await?;
        
        // NEW: Update index
        if let Ok(index) = SessionIndex::open_default() {
            if let Err(e) = index.index_session(path) {
                // Log warning but don't fail persist
                tracing::warn!("Failed to update session index: {}", e);
            }
        }
        
        Ok(())
    }
}
```

### Default Index Path
```rust
// src/session_index.rs

impl SessionIndex {
    pub fn open_default() -> Result<Self> {
        let path = dirs::data_dir()
            .unwrap_or_else(|| PathBuf::from("."))
            .join(".pi/agent/sessions/index.db");
        Self::open(&path)
    }
}
```

### Handle Index Creation
- Create index.db if not exists
- Use WAL mode for concurrent access
- Handle permission errors gracefully

## Considerations
1. **Performance**: Index update should be fast (<10ms)
2. **Reliability**: Don't fail persist if index fails
3. **Concurrency**: Handle multiple pi instances
4. **Migration**: Handle existing sessions without index entries

## Testing
- Test: persist() updates index
- Test: persist() succeeds even if index fails
- Test: Index contains session after persist

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Index created if not exists
- [ ] Errors logged but don't fail persist
- [ ] Performance impact <10ms","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:38:32.093344392Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T04:40:58.620167946Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3nz","depends_on_id":"bd-346","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:38:32.099125014Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-pwz","title":"Implement SSE streaming adapter for asupersync","description":"# Implement SSE streaming adapter for asupersync

## Goal
Bridge asupersync HTTP streaming response to the existing SSE parser (src/sse.rs).

## Background
The SSE parser in src/sse.rs is already complete and well-tested:
- SseParser::new() creates parser
- SseParser::feed(&str) returns Vec<SseEvent>
- Handles: event types, data fields, IDs, comments, multiline data
- 9 unit tests covering all edge cases

The challenge is connecting asupersync's streaming response body to this parser.

## Implementation

### Streaming Adapter
```rust
// src/http/sse.rs (new file or extend existing)

use crate::sse::{SseParser, SseEvent};
use asupersync::Cx;

pub struct SseStream {
    parser: SseParser,
    body: BodyReader,
    buffer: String,
}

impl SseStream {
    pub fn new(response: Response) -> Self {
        Self {
            parser: SseParser::new(),
            body: response.into_body(),
            buffer: String::new(),
        }
    }
    
    pub async fn next(&mut self, cx: &Cx) -> Option<Result<SseEvent, Error>> {
        loop {
            // Try to parse buffered data first
            let events = self.parser.feed(&self.buffer);
            if !events.is_empty() {
                self.buffer.clear();
                return Some(Ok(events.remove(0)));
            }
            
            // Read more data from body stream
            match self.body.read_chunk(cx).await {
                Ok(Some(chunk)) => {
                    self.buffer.push_str(&String::from_utf8_lossy(&chunk));
                }
                Ok(None) => return None,  // Stream ended
                Err(e) => return Some(Err(e.into())),
            }
        }
    }
}

// Or implement as a proper Stream trait for use with async iteration
impl Stream for SseStream {
    type Item = Result<SseEvent, Error>;
    
    fn poll_next(self: Pin<&mut Self>, cx: &mut Context<'_>) -> Poll<Option<Self::Item>> {
        // ...
    }
}
```

### Usage Pattern (in providers)
```rust
// In anthropic.rs
let response = client.post(url).json(&request).send(cx).await?;
let mut sse = SseStream::new(response);

while let Some(event) = sse.next(cx).await {
    match event? {
        SseEvent { data, event_type, .. } => {
            // Process Anthropic streaming event
        }
    }
}
```

## Considerations
1. **Backpressure**: Don't buffer entire response; process incrementally
2. **Error recovery**: Handle malformed events gracefully (log and continue)
3. **Cancellation**: Support clean shutdown via Cx cancellation
4. **Memory**: Limit buffer size to prevent OOM on malicious servers

## Dependencies
- bd-9sa (HTTP client wrapper must exist first)

## Testing
- Unit test: SseStream parses single event
- Unit test: SseStream handles multiline data
- Unit test: SseStream handles stream interruption
- Integration test: Stream from mock HTTP server

## Files
- src/http/sse.rs (new or extend)
- src/http/mod.rs (add re-export)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] SseStream wraps Response and yields SseEvent
- [ ] Incremental parsing works (no full buffering)
- [ ] Cancellation handled cleanly
- [ ] Memory bounded

```rust
fn create_styles(theme: &Theme) -> Styles {
    Styles {
        message_user: lipgloss::Style::new()
            .foreground(lipgloss::Color::hex(&theme.colors.accent)),
        
        message_assistant: lipgloss::Style::new()
            .foreground(lipgloss::Color::hex(&theme.colors.foreground)),

            .italic(true),
        
        error: lipgloss::Style::new()
            .foreground(lipgloss::Color::hex(&theme.colors.error))
            .bold(true),
        
        success: lipgloss::Style::new()
            .foreground(lipgloss::Color::hex(&theme.colors.success)),
        
        border: lipgloss::Style::new()
            .border_foreground(lipgloss::Color::hex(&theme.ui.border)),
        
        // ... more styles
    }
}
```

### Apply to PiApp
```rust
impl PiApp {
    pub fn new(config: Config) -> Self {
        let theme = config.theme()
            .map(|name| Theme::load_by_name(&name).ok())
            .flatten()
            .unwrap_or_else(Theme::dark);
        
        let styles = create_styles(&theme);
        
        Self {
            theme,
            styles,
            // ...
        }
    }
}
```

### Apply to Glamour (Markdown)
```rust
fn create_glamour_style(theme: &Theme) -> glamour::Style {
    glamour::Style {
        document: glamour::StyleBlock {
            color: Some(theme.colors.foreground.clone()),
            background_color: Some(theme.colors.background.clone()),
            ..Default::default()

        heading: glamour::StyleBlock {
            color: Some(theme.colors.accent.clone()),
            bold: Some(true),
            ..Default::default()
        },
        code: glamour::StyleBlock {
            color: Some(theme.syntax.string.clone()),
            ..Default::default()
        },
        // ... more elements
    }
}
```

-{"id":"bd-rhl","title":"Remove tokio and reqwest dependencies","description":"# Remove tokio and reqwest dependencies

## Goal
Complete the HTTP migration by removing tokio and reqwest from Cargo.toml.

## Background
After all providers migrate to asupersync, these dependencies become unused:
- tokio (async runtime)
- tokio-stream (stream utilities)
- reqwest (HTTP client)

This reduces binary size and simplifies the dependency tree.

## Implementation Steps

1. **Update Cargo.toml**
```toml
# Remove these lines:
tokio = { version = "1", features = ["full"] }
tokio-stream = "0.1"
reqwest = { version = "0.13", ... }

# Ensure asupersync is enabled (currently commented out):
asupersync = { path = "../asupersync", features = ["tls", "tls-native-roots", "sqlite"] }
```

2. **Update main.rs**
```rust
// Before
#[tokio::main]
async fn main() { ... }

// After - use asupersync runtime
fn main() {
    asupersync::runtime::Runtime::new()
        .block_on(async_main())
}
```

3. **Update all async code**
- Replace tokio::spawn with asupersync::spawn
- Replace tokio::time::sleep with asupersync equivalents
- Replace tokio::select! with asupersync patterns
- Update channel usage (if tokio channels used)

4. **Update test harness**
```rust
// Before
#[tokio::test]
async fn test_foo() { ... }

// After
#[asupersync::test]
async fn test_foo() { ... }
```

## Files Affected
- Cargo.toml
- src/main.rs
- src/agent.rs (spawn, select)
- src/tools.rs (bash timeout)
- All test files

## Dependencies
- bd-37l (Anthropic migrated)
- bd-1vo (OpenAI migrated)
- bd-1iy (Gemini migrated)
- bd-ivj (Azure migrated)

## Testing
- cargo build must succeed without tokio
- All tests must pass
- Binary size should decrease (~500KB-1MB)

## Risks
- Some tokio-specific features may need alternatives
- Test harness changes may be extensive

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tokio not in Cargo.toml
- [ ] reqwest not in Cargo.toml
- [ ] cargo build succeeds
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] Binary size reduced","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:33:32.853980666Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T03:33:42.951544591Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-rhl","depends_on_id":"bd-1iy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:33:42.693811991Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-rhl","depends_on_id":"bd-1vo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:33:42.436282419Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-rhl","depends_on_id":"bd-37l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:33:42.179760124Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-rhl","depends_on_id":"bd-gi3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:33:32.861092228Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-rhl","depends_on_id":"bd-ivj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:33:42.951519334Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
